zh.lifeSummary: 當Phebe Smith於1776年9月17日出生在美國紐約州普特南卡梅爾時，她的父親Elisha E Smith22歲，母親Rachel Hughson18歲。她於1794年與James Covey在美國紐約州格林結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她於1850年住在美國紐約州哈利素米特。她去世於1853年2月14日在美國紐約州格林劍橋大學默里 巴爾的摩劍橋大學默里 巴爾的摩享年76歲，埋葬在美國紐約州格林劍橋大學默里 巴爾的摩格拉維爾 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Phebe Smith于1776年9月17日出生在美国纽约州普特南卡梅爾时，她的父亲Elisha E Smith22岁，母亲Rachel Hughson18岁。她于1794年与James Covey在美国纽约州格林结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她于1850年住在美国纽约州哈利素米特。她去世于1853年2月14日在美国纽约州格林劍橋大學默里 巴爾的摩劍橋大學默里 巴爾的摩享年76岁，埋葬在美国纽约州格林劍橋大學默里 巴爾的摩格拉維爾 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Smith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

variants.name: Smither
meanings: English (Surrey): occupational name from Middle English smith(i)ere, smeth(i)ere ‘blacksmith’, a derivative of Middle English smithen, smethen, ‘to make (something) in metal, to forge’. Compare Smith .

variants.name: Smithey
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from Middle English smithi ‘smithy, forge’ (Old Norse smithja), used either as a topographic name for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, or an occupational name for someone who worked in one. Compare Smith and Smead .

variants.name: Smyth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Ronan, Aidan, Brigid, Conor, Eoin, Malachy, Paddy.
English: variant of   Smith  . This form of the surname is particularly common Ireland.

variants.name: McGowan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Siobhan, Cormac, Seamus, Aileen, Colm, Eithne, Kieran, Niall, Paddy.

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (a') Ghobhainn or Mac Gobhann (Scottish) or Mac (an) Ghabhann (Irish), ‘son of (the) smith’. This name has also been Anglicized as Smith and Smithson .

variants.name: Smead
meanings: occupational name from Middle English smeth(e) ‘smith’, a phonetic variant of Smith 1 that was widespread in medieval England, especially in south-eastern and south-western counties.

topographic, occupational, and habitational name from Middle English smethe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smethe), a side form of Smith 2, for someone who lived and worked at a blacksmith's, or who lived in or came from a place so named, such as Smeeth (Kent) or Smeed Farm in Hastingleigh (Kent). Compare the synonymous Smithey .

variants.name: Faber
meanings: German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), and Danish; Slovak and Czech (mainly Fáber): occupational name from Latin faber ‘blacksmith’. At the time of the Reformation, it was much used as a humanistic name, a translation into Latin of vernacular surnames such as German Schmidt and Dutch Smit . Compare Fabri and Fauber .

History: In North America, this surname was originally also part of a title. It was François Lefebvre, sieur Duplessis Faber from Paris, France, whose title sieur Duplessis Faber was shortened into the surname Faber. He married Marie-Madeleine Chorel in Champlain, QC, in 1689.

historicEvents.title: The First Constitution
historicEvents.description: Serving the newly created United States of America as the first constitution, the Articles of Confederation were an agreement among the 13 original states preserving the independence and sovereignty of the states. But with a limited central government, the Constitutional Convention came together to replace the Articles of Confederation with a more established Constitution and central government on where the states can be represented and voice their concerns and comments to build up the nation.
Historical Boundaries 1795: Schoharie, New York, United States

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.
Oldest grave seen in memorials list

historicEvents.title: Panic! of 1819
historicEvents.description: With the Aftermath of the Napoleonic Wars the global market for trade was down. During this time, America had its first financial crisis and it lasted for only two years.

historicEvents.title: Slavery Becomes Illegal in New York State
historicEvents.description: During the years 1799 to 1827, New York went through a period of gradual emancipation. A Gradual Emancipation Law was passed in 1799 which freed slave children born after July 4, 1799. However, they were indentured until 25 years old for women and 28 years old for men. A law passed 1817 which freed slaves born before 1799, yet delayed their emancipation for ten years. All remaining slaves were freed in New York State on July 4, 1827.
U.S. acquires vast tracts of Mexican territory in wake of Mexican War including California and New Mexico.

en.lifeSummary: When Phebe Smith was born on 17 September 1776, in Carmel, Putnam, New York, United States, her father, Elisha E Smith, was 22 and her mother, Rachel Hughson, was 18. She married James Covey in 1794, in Greene, New York, United States. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. She lived in Summit, Schoharie, New York, United States in 1850. She died on 14 February 1853, in New Baltimore, New Baltimore, Greene, New York, United States, at the age of 76, and was buried in Grapeville Cemetery, New Baltimore, Greene, New York, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Phebe Smith est née le 17 septembre 1776, à Carmel, Putnam, New York, États-Unis, son père, Elisha E Smith, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Rachel Hughson, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé James Covey en 1794, à Greene, New York, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. Elle a vécu à Summit, Schoharie, New York, États-Unis en 1850. Elle est décédée le 14 février 1853, à New Baltimore, New Baltimore, Greene, New York, États-Unis, à l’âge de 76 ans, et a été enterrée à Grapeville Cemetery, New Baltimore, Greene, New York, États-Unis.

Sie hat 1794 in Greene, New York, Vereinigte Staaten James Covey geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. 1850 hat sie in Summit, Schoharie, New York, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. Sie starb am 14. Februar 1853 im Alter von 76 Jahren in New Baltimore, New Baltimore, Greene, New York, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Grapeville Cemetery, New Baltimore, Greene, New York, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Phebe Smith è nata il il 17 settembre 1776 nel Carmel, Putnam, New York, Stati Uniti d'America, sua padre, Elisha E Smith, aveva 22 anni e sua madre, Rachel Hughson, aveva 18 anni. Lei sposato James Covey nel 1794, nel Greene, New York, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie. Vissuto a Summit, Schoharie, New York, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1850. Morì il 14 febbraio 1853, nel New Baltimore, New Baltimore, Greene, New York, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 76 anni, e fu sepolta a Grapeville Cemetery, New Baltimore, Greene, New York, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Phebe Smithが1776年9月17日にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州プウトナムカーメルで生まれた時，父Elisha E Smithは22歳，母Rachel Hughsonは18歳であった。1794年にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州グレエンでJames Coveyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1850年にはアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州スチオハーイスンミトに住んでいた。彼女は1853年2月14日にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州グレエンニュー ボーティモーニュー ボーティモーで76歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州グレエンニュー ボーティモーグラペヴィル セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.New Baltimore, Greene, New York, United: 미국 뉴욕 주 그린 뉴 바팀어 뉴 바팀어

ko.lifeSummary: Phebe Smith(이)가 1776년 9월 17일 미국 뉴욕 주 풋남 카멀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Elisha E Smith(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Rachel Hughson(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 1794년 미국 뉴욕 주 그린에서 James Covey(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1850년도에 미국 뉴욕 주 슈해리 수미에서 살았다. 그녀는 1853년 2월 14일 미국 뉴욕 주 그린 뉴 바팀어 뉴 바팀어에서 76세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 뉴욕 주 그린 뉴 바팀어 그라p에빌 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Phebe Smith nasceu em 17 de setembro de 1776, em Carmel, Putnam, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Elisha E Smith, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Rachel Hughson, tinha 18 anos. Ela casou-se com James Covey em 1794, em Greene, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela viveu em Summit, Schoharie, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América em 1850. Ela faleceu em 14 de fevereiro de 1853, em New Baltimore, New Baltimore, Greene, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América, com 76 anos, e foi sepultada em Grapeville Cemetery, New Baltimore, Greene, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.New Baltimore, Green, New York, United S: ню балтимор, ню балтимор, грин, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Phebe Smith родилась 17 сентября 1776 г. в местности кармел, путнам, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени Elisha E Smith было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Rachel Hughson, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени James Covey в 1794 г. в местности грин, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она жила в местности суммит, щохари, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты в 1850 году. Она умерла 14 февраля 1853 г. в местности ню балтимор, ню балтимор, грин, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 76 лет и была похоронена в местности грапевилл кеметири, ню балтимор, грин, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Phebe Smith nació el 17 de septiembre de 1776, en Carmel, Putnam, Nueva York, Estados Unidos, su papá, Elisha E Smith, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Rachel Hughson, tenía 18 años. Se casó con James Covey en 1794, en Greene, Nueva York, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas. Vivió en Summit, Schoharie, Nueva York, Estados Unidos en 1850. Murió el 14 de febrero de 1853, en New Baltimore, New Baltimore, Greene, Nueva York, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 76 años, y fue sepultada en Grapeville Cemetery, New Baltimore, Greene, Nueva York, Estados Unidos.
